line1: Most businesses don't have an AI problem. They have a clarity problem.
line2: They know what they want to achieve. They don't know how to make AI serve that — without losing what makes them distinctive.
line3: We don't arrive with tools and templates. We arrive with questions — then build the framework around your answers.
line4: If you've opened ten AI tools and closed all ten without using any of them, you're not behind. You're just missing the framework, not the tools.
